query: How do CD4+ and CD8+ T cells regulate macrophage polarization and bone healing around titanium implants?

naive_rag.query: How do CD4+ and CD8+ T cells regulate macrophage polarization and bone healing around titanium implants?
naive_rag.synthesis:
CD4+ and CD8+ T cells are essential for modulating the peri-implant microenvironment, reducing inflammation, and promoting bone healing around titanium implants (@avery23, @avery24, @avery25).
Deficiency of either CD4+ or CD8+ T cell subsets alters macrophage polarization and reduces MSC recruitment and proliferation at the implantation site (@avery23, @avery24, @avery25).
CD4+ T cells play a significant immunomodulatory role in neutrophil clearance, macrophage polarization, MSC recruitment/proliferation, and promoting bone-implant contact (@avery23, @avery24, @avery25).
CD4+ T cells (Th2 cells and Tregs) activate anti-inflammatory macrophages and suppress pro-inflammatory ones via IL-4 and IL-10 secretion (@avery23, @avery24, @avery25).
CD4+ T cells are a significant source of anti-inflammatory cytokines; CD4+-deficient mice demonstrated decreased IL-4 production and lower anti-inflammatory macrophage populations (@avery23, @avery24, @avery25).
